metadata.summary: Instagram strategy prioritizing Reels (80%) for discovery, Stories (15%) for engagement, Feed (5%). Organic reach declined; without Reels, accounts plateau.

content.title: Instagram Basics
content.summary: Instagram strategy prioritizing Reels (80%) for discovery, Stories (15%) for engagement, Feed (5%). Without Reels, accounts plateau. Profile optimization, pillar discipline, and hashtag minimalism foundational.

why.p: Instagram has 2B+ monthly active users, and Reels deliver 2x the reach of photos by pushing to non-followers. Accounts that ignore Reels get roughly 1/4 the reach of Reels-led accounts. The first 1.5 seconds of a Reel determine algorithmic reach; 80% of viewers watch muted — on-screen text is mandatory. DM automation requires ManyChat approval; unofficial automation risks account suspension.

list.item: Setting up an Instagram presence (founder, brand, creator) and configuring profile, highlights, and content pillars.
Reels-first content strategy where the team can shoot/edit but needs scripts, hooks, and carousel scripts.
Auditing an underperforming Instagram account to identify whether decline is reach, engagement, or conversion.
Drafting carousel scripts (10 slides) and Story sequences for product launches.
Pairing with Meta Ads where organic posts feed retargeting audiences.
Audience is purely B2B enterprise — LinkedIn and email outperform; do not invest cycles here.
Team cannot shoot or edit video; Instagram without Reels gets roughly 1/4 the reach of a Reels-led account.
Brand requires text-heavy, long-form content (use blog + LinkedIn).
Regulated industries where every visual claim needs compliance review incompatible with Instagram's posting velocity.

rule.statement: Structure the Instagram bio as: line 1 = what you do for whom; line 2 = credibility/proof; line 3 = CTA.
rule.rationale: Visitors decide to follow in under 3 seconds. A three-line bio that answers "what is this account, why should I trust it, and what do I do next" converts profile visits to followers more reliably than a clever but ambiguous bio.

list.item: Display name: include a keyword (e.g., "Mike | SaaS Growth").
Profile photo: face clearly visible, bright, consistent with other platforms.
Highlights: About, Products/Services, Testimonials, FAQ, Free Resources.

rule.statement: Allocate 80% of content effort to Reels; write the hook separately and only ship Reels with hook score 4+/5.
rule.rationale: Reels deliver 2x the reach of photos by pushing to non-followers. The first 1.5 seconds determine algorithmic reach — a weak hook wastes the production effort. Rating hooks explicitly forces quality discipline.

rule.statement: Always include on-screen text in Reels; never rely on voiceover-only scripts.
rule.rationale: 80% of Instagram viewers watch muted. Scripts that put all content in voiceover fail to communicate to the majority of viewers.

list.item: Optimal Reels length: 7-15 seconds for virality; 30-60 seconds for educational depth.

rule.statement: Post Stories daily; pair every Reel with a Story re-share within 24 hours.
rule.rationale: Stories maintain relationship with existing followers while Reels reach new ones. Algorithm rewards accounts that use multiple formats consistently.

rule.statement: Use 3-5 hashtags per post (mixed small/medium/large tiers). Never request 30 hashtags from an agent.
rule.rationale: Meta now favors topic understanding over hashtag density. Over-indexing on hashtags underperforms and can look spammy. Agent prompts that request 30 hashtags produce stale, low-quality tag lists.

antipattern.reason: Instagram suppresses reach for Reels that contain the TikTok watermark. Cross-posted content must have the watermark stripped and ideally be re-edited for Instagram's format.

rule.statement: Caption hook must appear in the first line (before "more"); sweet spot length 150-200 characters; always include CTA.
rule.rationale: Instagram truncates captions after the first line in the feed view. A hook in the first line pulls viewers to expand. Captions over 2,200 characters are permitted but rarely read in full.

rule.statement: Enforce a max-words-per-slide constraint for carousel scripts; reject slide 1 with more than 10 words.
rule.rationale: Carousel slides with 30+ words on slide 1 fail because viewers swipe before reading. Large, readable text on a simple visual stops the scroll.

rule.statement: Only use approved ManyChat flows for DM automation. Never use unofficial APIs to auto-send DMs at scale.
rule.rationale: Instagram restricts DM automation to approved partners (Messenger Platform). Unapproved bots get accounts flagged or suspended. Agents must never send DMs via unofficial APIs regardless of scale.

antipattern.description: LLM-generated captions with heavy exclamation marks and emojis.
antipattern.reason: LLMs default to enthusiastic, emoji-heavy Instagram captions. This reads as inauthentic and degrades brand credibility. Explicitly prohibit exclamation marks and emojis (or set a strict per-post limit) in every prompt.

antipattern.description: Agent picks trending hashtags from training data.
antipattern.reason: Hashtag trends change weekly. Training-data hashtag suggestions are stale. Fetch current top hashtags via a research subagent or manual lookup before generating content.

antipattern.description: All Reel scripts follow listicle structure ("3 tips for X").
antipattern.reason: LLMs default to listicle format. Overuse of listicles reduces content variety and engagement. Force narrative or transformation arcs periodically to avoid audience fatigue.

antipattern.description: Agent recommends "trending audio" without an external lookup.
antipattern.reason: The model cannot know current trending audio on Instagram — it has no real-time data. Require an external lookup before the agent recommends audio choices.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: A Claude subagent is best at scripting Reels (hook + body + CTA in 30 seconds), drafting carousel scripts (slide-by-slide), and proposing weekly Story sequences. The agent should NOT auto-post Reels (visual QA matters) but CAN schedule via Buffer/Later if approved by the operator. Pair the agent with a research subagent that pulls trending audio and competitor recent winners daily.

Best practices: Pillar discipline beats clever ideas — enforce the agent's prompt to label every draft with a pillar; reject pillar-less output. Reels' first 1.5 seconds decide reach. Force the agent to write the hook separately and rate it 1-5; only ship 4+. Always include on-screen text — 80% of viewers watch muted; agents that produce voiceover-only scripts fail. Hashtag minimalism: 3-5 mixed tiers, not 30. Agent prompts that ask for "30 hashtags" hurt performance. Save a "winners library" of past top posts and inject the top 5 into every ideation prompt as positive examples. Pair every Reel with a Story re-share within 24h; agent should produce both in the same content brief.

section.name: tools-and-services
section.p: CLI tools for content operations: instagrapi (Python, read profile and insights — unofficial, breakage risk), instaloader (backup posts/stories/highlights for analysis), ffmpeg (trim, resize 9:16, strip watermarks), imagemagick (carousel slide generation from markdown).

SaaS platforms: Instagram Graph API (official, Reels + posts publishing for Business/Creator accounts), Buffer (Reels + carousel + Stories scheduling via API), Later (visual planner; preview grid before posting), Metricool (analytics + scheduling, hashtag analyzer), Iconosquare (deeper analytics, competitor tracking), ManyChat (approved DM automation flows), Canva (programmatic carousel generation), Postiz (OSS scheduler with Instagram support), CapCut (Reels editing — manual; agents can output edit scripts).

section.name: api-constraints
section.p: Organic reach is structurally low (often 1-3% of followers for photos) and shrinking — without Reels, growth stalls. API limits posting flexibility: Reels can be scheduled via Graph API, but Stories with stickers (polls, quizzes) often cannot. Hashtag strategy has weakened; Meta now favors topic understanding, so over-indexing on hashtag tiers underperforms. DM automation must be approved (Messenger Platform); unapproved bots get the account flagged. Cross-posting Reels from TikTok with the watermark suppresses reach; agents must strip and remix.

template.purpose: Three-line bio formula with credibility and CTA.
Hook + body + CTA Reels script template.
Prompt for generating 5 Reels scripts with pillar tags and hook scores.
